---
title: "Genome-wide discovery of T-box riboswitches and expansion of their known phylogenetic distribution"
author: "bioedca"
abstract: |
  *[Abstract — placeholder. Written at the preprint/submission gate once the
  Phase 4/5 discovery result and its GATE-1/GATE-2 evidence are in hand. No
  result is stated here at Phase 0.]*
format:
  typst:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
---

<!--
Living manuscript. One paragraph + figure caption is added at each phase-exit
gate (CLAUDE.md §6.2/§6.4); an unwritten paragraph is a phase-exit blocker.
Every biological/statistical fact carries a machine-traceable citation inline
(PMID/DOI + accessed date, CLAUDE.md §10.1). No metric, discovery count, or
eval number is asserted before the phase that measures it (§10.3). A formatted
bibliography is generated from these identifiers at the release gate; rendered
with Typst (no LaTeX in the toolchain).
-->

# Introduction

*[Placeholder — authored across Phases 4–6 as the discovery narrative lands.]*

# Methods

## Data provenance and curation {#sec-data}

The training corpus comprises **23,535** curated T-box riboswitch records drawn
from the T-box database (TBDB) lineage (DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa721; accessed
2026-07-11), re-ingested through a proper-CSV parse gate that reconciles the
record count and per-record hash-identity against the upstream cleaned table
before any downstream use. Each record's taxonomy is re-placed onto a single
pinned GTDB release so that the clade boundaries used for splitting are
internally consistent. Notably, the curated corpus contains **no archaeal
records**; whether *bona fide* T-boxes exist in Archaea (and in the
under-sampled DPANN, CPR/Patescibacteria, and candidate phyla) is treated as an
open question the genome-wide scan is designed to test, not a settled negative
(PMID:36594112, DOI:10.1002/wrna.1774; PMID:31020937,
DOI:10.2174/1386207322666190425143301; accessed 2026-07-11). Dataset
construction, splits, and evaluation are reported following the DOME
recommendations for machine learning in biology (DOI:10.1093/gigascience/giae094,
PMID:39661723; accessed 2026-07-10).

## Non-circular evaluation design {#sec-eval}

Because the headline claim is a **generalization** claim — that a learned model
recovers T-boxes beyond the phylogenetic range of its training data — homology
leakage across the train/test boundary would inflate exactly the metric that is
the paper's central result. We therefore partition the corpus by a
**structure-aware, homology-clustered leave-clade-out** scheme: positives are
single-linkage clustered on consensus-column identity over per-class covariance-
model alignments (**RF00230** for class I, **TBDB001** for class II, applied
within each class and unioned), whole clusters are assigned to folds, and
clade-crossing clusters are held out, yielding a partition with maximum
held-out-to-training
consensus identity **< 0.70** (observed maximum 0.699). This design is motivated
by the documented collapse of sequence-based homology search on divergent
non-coding RNA (PMID:17151342, DOI:10.1101/gr.5890907; PMID:19861422,
DOI:10.1261/rna.1556009; accessed 2026-07-10), which is precisely the regime in
which a generalization claim must be tested. Leakage control is enforced as a
**CI-blocking test** over the real full-corpus partition on every change.

Detection is evaluated against pre-registered, blinded-frozen gates: **GATE-1**
(recall at matched precision versus a `cmsearch` baseline, with a two-part
effect-size-plus-CI-floor bar over block-resampled, macro-averaged arms),
**GATE-2** (in-distribution calibration error and a bootstrap-CI-bounded
discovery false-discovery proportion on a conservative empirical null),
**GATE-3** (a per-corpus verdict rolled up to a project-level outcome), and
**GATE-4** (per-nucleotide segmentation F1 taken as the **minimum over the three
core structural elements**, not a mean). Circularity is further controlled by a
class-II-CM-naive detector checkpoint that is never routed through the covariance
model it would otherwise memorize, and by requiring **orthogonal** (covariation,
architecture, synteny) evidence before any novel-clade discovery is confirmed.
All gate thresholds are pinned across six Architecture Decision Records
(ADR-0001…0006) and are frozen prior to unblinding.

::: {#fig-splits}
*[Figure — split-separation diagnostic. Placeholder image inserted at render
time.]*

**Figure 1. The leave-clade-out split admits no train/test homology leakage.**
All-versus-all consensus-column identity between held-out and training T-box
positives, computed over per-class covariance-model alignments (RF00230 for
class I, TBDB001 for class II). The mass of held-out
records lies below the 0.70 clustering cut (identity ≥ 0.70 links records into a
single cluster assigned wholly to one fold), so that **no** held-out record
shares ≥ 0.70 identity with any training record (observed maximum 0.699). The
tighter-cutoff sensitivity sweep is stable and conservative under tightening.
:::

# Results

*[Placeholder — GATE-4 (Phase 2), GATE-2 (Phase 3), GATE-1 (Phase 4), the
genome-scale scan + FDR (Phase 5), and orthogonal validation / GATE-3 (Phase 6)
paragraphs are added at each phase-exit gate.]*

# Discussion

*[Placeholder — authored at the preprint gate.]*
